user_message: Robert Petrov at Woodgrove Labs just sent this. Help me run the hygiene check.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 10:18 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with escalation triage. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
Date: 2024-02-28 14:03 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our CFO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 13:15 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Woodgrove Labs: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-E trap (MSX proposals duplicate already-tracked items); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-E failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-E failure mode (MSX proposals duplicate already-tracked items)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-E -- MSX proposals duplicate already-tracked items
